name: intersect_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_intersect_always_empty(cm1: CommitMask, cm2: CommitMask, res: CommitMask)
    requires
        res@ == cm1@.intersect(cm2@),
    ensures
        res@ =~= Set::<int>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Would mean every intersection is vacuously empty regardless of inputs, indicating a trivial spec

name: self_intersect_empty
code:
proof fn phi_2_self_intersect_empty(cm: CommitMask, res: CommitMask)
    requires
        res@ == cm@.intersect(cm@),
    ensures
        res@ =~= Set::<int>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Self-intersection must equal self; if it equals empty the spec loses all set information at boundary

name: result_leaks_outside_other
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_leaks_outside_other(cm1: CommitMask, cm2: CommitMask, res: CommitMask, i: int)
    requires
        res@ == cm1@.intersect(cm2@),
        res@.contains(i),
    ensures
        !cm2@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: An intersection element not in the second operand violates the fundamental intersection contract

name: intersect_equals_union
code:
proof fn phi_4_intersect_equals_union(cm1: CommitMask, cm2: CommitMask, res: CommitMask)
    requires
        res@ == cm1@.intersect(cm2@),
    ensures
        res@ =~= cm1@.union(cm2@),
{
}
reason: Conflating intersection with union would make the result a superset instead of a subset of operands

name: intersect_no_filtering
code:
proof fn phi_5_intersect_no_filtering(cm1: CommitMask, cm2: CommitMask, res: CommitMask, i: int)
    requires
        res@ == cm1@.intersect(cm2@),
        cm1@.contains(i),
    ensures
        res@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: Would mean intersection never filters anything from self, making it equivalent to identity on the first operand
